input_messages.content: {"suggestions": ["Segment the compromised UbuntuComputer into a restricted VLAN/zone (micro-segmentation) and enforce firewall policies that only permit minimal required egress (management/monitoring) while denying bulk/unsanctioned protocols; keep the host on the network but limit its ability to send high-volume ICMP externally.", "Enable control-plane protection (CoPP/CPU protection) on the Cisco switch and FortiGate to rate-limit and drop excessive ICMP and other high-rate packets destined to network devices so infrastructure remains responsive during the flood.", "Apply a strict egress protocol allowlist on the FortiGate (default-deny for LAN\u2192WAN) so only business-essential protocols are permitted outbound; this reduces the attack surface by preventing non-essential protocols (including ICMP where not required) from leaving the LAN.", "Deploy Squid as an enforced outbound proxy for web traffic and combine it with firewall egress controls to limit all non-proxied outbound traffic, reducing opportunities to abuse arbitrary protocols from compromised hosts.", "Increase detection and automated response: enable and tune FortiGate IPS/DoS sensors and flow-logging to trigger automated throttles or dynamic policy changes when abnormal ICMP volume is detected, and feed alerts into monitoring for rapid human review."], "chosen_mitigation": "Segment the compromised UbuntuComputer into a restricted VLAN/zone (micro-segmentation) and enforce firewall policies that only permit minimal required egress (management/monitoring) while denying bulk/unsanctioned protocols; keep the host on the network but limit its ability to send high-volume ICMP externally.", "no_mitigation_left": false, "current_mitigations_counter": 2, "reached_mitigation_limit": false, "mitigation_start_timestamp": "2026-03-12T14:25:19.811431"}

{
  "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Segment the compromised UbuntuComputer into a restricted VLAN/zone (micro-segmentation) and enforce firewall policies that only permit minimal required egress (management/monitoring) while denying bulk/unsanctioned protocols; keep the host on the network but limit its ability to send high-volume ICMP externally.",
  "comments": "I implemented host-level micro-segmentation via the FortiGate firewall. On the FortiGate I created address objects for the compromised Ubuntu host and the Caldera server, defined minimal allowed services (Caldera TCP/8443 and DNS UDP/53), and applied explicit firewall policies that: 1) deny PING (ICMP) originating from the UbuntuComputer to anywhere, 2) allow only TCP/8443 from the UbuntuComputer to the Caldera server, and 3) allow DNS UDP from the UbuntuComputer. Logging was enabled on the policies for monitoring and audit. This preserves the compromised host in-network (attacker still present) but stops its ability to perform high-volume ICMP floods to external targets (the primary attack vector observed), reducing the attack success while allowing only required management/monitoring traffic. During execution a minor FortiGate parse warning appeared when defining the UDP service, but I validated the final address/service/policy configuration with show commands and confirmed the deny/allow policies are present and active.",
  "device_identifier": "FortiGate7.0.14-1",
  "commands": [
    "config firewall address\nedit \"UbuntuComputer\"\n set subnet 192.168.1.3 255.255.255.255\nnext\nedit \"Caldera_Server\"\n set subnet 203.0.113.1 255.255.255.255\nnext\nend\n\nconfig firewall service custom\nedit \"CALDERA_8443\"\n set protocol TCP\n set tcp-portrange 8443\nnext\nedit \"DNS_udp\"\n set protocol UDP\n set udp-portrange 53\nnext\nend\n\nconfig firewall policy\nedit 1\n set name \"Deny_Ubuntu_ICMP\"\n set srcintf \"port2\"\n set dstintf \"port1\"\n set srcaddr \"UbuntuComputer\"\n set dstaddr \"all\"\n set action deny\n set service \"PING\"\n set schedule \"always\"\n set logtraffic all\nnext\nedit 2\n set name \"Allow_Ubuntu_Caldera_8443\"\n set srcintf \"port2\"\n set dstintf \"port1\"\n set srcaddr \"UbuntuComputer\"\n set dstaddr \"Caldera_Server\"\n set action accept\n set service \"CALDERA_8443\"\n set nat enable\n set schedule \"always\"\n set logtraffic all\nnext\nedit 3\n set name \"Allow_Ubuntu_DNS\"\n set srcintf \"port2\"\n set dstintf \"port1\"\n set srcaddr \"UbuntuComputer\"\n set dstaddr \"all\"\n set action accept\n set service \"DNS_udp\"\n set schedule \"always\"\n set logtraffic all\nnext\nend\n\nconfig firewall policy\nshow\nend\n\nconfig firewall address\nshow\nend"
  ]
}

response.content: {"comment": "Assessment summary: The implemented FortiGate firewall changes match the intended mitigation (restrict the compromised UbuntuComputer's egress and block ICMP floods) and the key blocking rule is present and highest priority. Evidence: a deny policy named \"Deny_Ubuntu_ICMP\" (policy 1) exists with service set to \"PING\" and source address set to UbuntuComputer; two allow policies for Caldera (TCP/8443) and DNS are present. Strengths: policy ordering places the ICMP deny before the allow rules, which should stop the observed hping3 ICMP flood from the agent while preserving allowed management traffic. Issues and validation notes: 1) A parse error occurred when running the service creation for DNS_udp (command parse error before 'UDP'). Although the policy shows DNS_udp in use, the parse error indicates the DNS custom service may not have its protocol field correctly set; confirm the DNS_udp service matches UDP/53 so DNS traffic is actually permitted. 2) You used interface names \"port2\"/\"port1\" \u2014 FortiGate accepted them, but please validate these map to the LAN (Ubuntu) and WAN (Internet/Caldera path) as expected so the rules apply in the correct direction. 3) Confirm by testing from the Ubuntu host that: (a) ping/hping3 to 7.0.14.254 fails (attack traffic blocked), (b) the agent can still reach Caldera on TCP/8443, and (c) DNS resolution works if needed. Overall decision: the core mitigation (blocking ICMP egress from the compromised host while allowing minimal required traffic) has been implemented and is effective for stopping the active ICMP flood; the minor service parse error and interface-mapping verification should be addressed, but they do not negate the primary mitigation.", "approved": true, "mitigation_currently_implemented": "Segment the compromised UbuntuComputer into a restricted VLAN/zone (micro-segmentation) and enforce firewall policies that only permit minimal required egress (management/monitoring) while denying bulk/unsanctioned protocols; keep the host on the network but limit its ability to send high-volume ICMP externally.", "mitigations_counter": 2, "mitigation_iteration": 1, "reached_mitigation_iteration_limit": false}

additional_info.current_mitigation: Segment the compromised UbuntuComputer into a restricted VLAN/zone (micro-segmentation) and enforce firewall policies that only permit minimal required egress (management/monitoring) while denying bulk/unsanctioned protocols; keep the host on the network but limit its ability to send high-volume ICMP externally.
